repository.name: nanoclaw-todo-agent
repository.description: 🤖 智能待办 Agent skill for NanoClaw — AI-powered todo management via Telegram

commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  respond to the .dark class (set by next-themes) instead of OS media query

  correctly by most recent

commit.message:
fix: use %{%} (% register) to get current filename in :sh

%{filename} is not a valid expansion in Helix :sh commands.
In Helix, the % register holds the current buffer's filename
and is accessible via %{%} in typed command expansions.

commit.message:
Fix: auto-open Clinicea profile for untagged calls + add call chain logging

Root cause: when monitor sends empty Agent, server sets agent=null.
Frontend check 'data.agent === myUsername' was 'null === agent1' = false,
so profile never auto-opened for agents on untagged calls.

Fix: shouldAutoOpen now includes '|| !data.agent' — untagged calls
open for all logged-in agents (same as isEventForMe allows them).

Also added:
- Server: log raw payload on POST /incoming_call (From, Agent, IP)
- Server: log emitted rooms and cliniceaUrl
- PS1: log 'CALL DETECTED' with full notification text
- PS1: retry webhook POST 3x with 2s delay
- PS1: log webhook response body
- PS1: log notification parse errors (was silent catch{})

context:

ai-role: implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add async: true to SessionEnd hook to prevent upload cancellation

Race condition confirmed: 6/150 sessions (4%) silently fail when Claude Code
kills the hook subprocess before completion. Async detaches from parent
signal group so the upload survives session teardown.

Refs DaveX2001/deliverable-tracking#1096

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add full Docker image with debugging tools (#71)

## Summary
- Rename the current minimal `output` Dockerfile stage to `slim`; add a
new `output` stage that layers debugging tools (`procps`, `vim-tiny`,
`less`, `curl`, `net-tools`, `iputils-ping`, `htop`, `lsof`, `strace`)
on top
- Add `*_slim` bake targets in `docker-bake.hcl` for all PG versions
(14-18)
- Update CI to build and push both variants; merged tags: `18-main`
(full, default) and `18-main-slim`

## Test plan
- [ ] CI docker build passes for both full and slim targets
- [ ] `docker run` the full image and verify `ps`, `vim`, `free`, `top`,
`htop` are available
- [ ] `docker run` the slim image and verify it behaves identically to
the previous image

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: Enable TLS unit tests in CI by setting ARROW_TEST_DATA and regenerating keystores

The TLS tests (ConnectionTlsTest, ConnectionTlsRootCertsTest,
ConnectionMutualTlsTest) were failing because ARROW_TEST_DATA was not
set and the JKS keystores were stale. CI now generates keystores from
the arrow-testing submodule certs and sets ARROW_TEST_DATA.

Also treats UNAUTHENTICATED as benign during CloseSession, since
unauthenticated connections cannot close server-side sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pin bcrypt==3.2.2 to fix passlib compatibility

passlib 1.7.4 detect_wrap_bug() uses a >72 byte password, which bcrypt 4.x
now rejects with ValueError. Pinning to 3.2.2 resolves the startup crash.

repository.description: Bash and Zsh shell script recorders for CodeTracer

commit.message:
feat: replace OpenAI built-in voice with ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 TTS

Architecture change in the Realtime pipeline:
- OpenAI Realtime still handles STT + LLM + generates audio internally
  (audio generation is kept so response.audio_transcript.done fires)
- response.audio.delta events are now suppressed — no longer forwarded
  to Twilio
- On response.audio_transcript.done, the full transcript is piped to
  ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 via streaming HTTP with output_format=ulaw_8000
- ElevenLabs audio chunks (40ms each) are base64-encoded and streamed
  directly to Twilio as media events — zero codec conversion needed
- Barge-in cancels the in-flight ElevenLabs asyncio.Task instantly,
  stopping audio mid-stream and clearing the Twilio buffer as before

New env vars: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID (both documented
in .env.example; ELEVENLABS_API_KEY must be set on Render)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Pre-Submit Review section to CLAUDE.md

Codifies the deep-review expectation before committing: no heuristic
shortcuts, no redundancy, long-term correctness, docs in sync, and
byte-compile clean. Sits above Quality Checks as the higher-level gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disambiguator infinite loop — only offer each broad parent once per session

The disambiguator fired on every turn as long as dance-multi (or any
broad parent) remained in merged_params. Typing refinements like
'what about overnight?' still inherited dance-multi from session
context, re-triggering the picker indefinitely and blocking all searches.

Fix: track offered parents in st.session_state['_disambiguated_tags'].
Once a parent has been shown, skip it on subsequent turns. The user
either clicked a child (replacing the tag) or bypassed it — either way
the search should proceed.

_disambiguated_tags is cleared on Surprise Me resets so fresh broad
searches start clean. It persists within the session otherwise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tracing-based debug logging (#231)

Doco currently has zero logging infrastructure — just two println! calls in the
entire codebase. When something goes wrong during test execution (container
startup, WebDriver connection, networking), users have no visibility into what
the framework is doing.

This adds structured logging via `tracing` so users can set
`RUST_LOG=doco=debug` and see exactly what's happening. The default filter is
`doco=info,warn`, which keeps Doco's info-level messages visible (ephemeral
environment init, selenium startup) without noise from dependencies.

The `#[doco::main]` macro now emits a `doco::init_tracing()` call before the
test runner starts, so logging is available from the very beginning of
execution. The init function uses `try_init()` so it's safe to call multiple
times.

Instrumentation covers the full lifecycle: service and server container startup,
bridge IP resolution, WebDriver connection, browser viewport configuration,
navigation, and session teardown. Each test also runs inside an `info_span!`
with the test name, so log lines are attributable to specific tests.

An integration test exercises the full pipeline by running a minimal Doco suite
(Caddy responding with "Hello World") and asserting that key lifecycle messages
appear in stderr.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(prompts): separate operational rules from personality defaults

Identity now contains only non-negotiable operational rules (act don't
suggest, close loops, fix mistakes, consult lessons, confirm externals).

Soul is split into two clearly labeled blocks:
- Defaults: personality traits overridden by learned lessons
- Boundaries: non-negotiable safety rules lessons cannot override

This eliminates ~40% of dead-weight tokens for users with established
lessons, while keeping the same behavior for new users.

commit.message:
Fix simultaneous monitor disconnects across all agents

Root causes found and fixed:
1. agentHeartbeats is in-memory only — every server restart wiped all state,
   marking ALL monitors dead simultaneously. Added 2-minute startup grace
   period so monitors aren't marked dead until they've had time to reconnect.
2. Stale threshold was 45s (too tight) — increased to 90s to survive server
   restarts and network hiccups without false disconnects.
3. No process crash handlers — added uncaughtException/unhandledRejection
   handlers to prevent unhandled errors from crashing the server.
4. PS1 heartbeat had no retry — silent catch{} meant a single failed POST
   caused a 30s gap. Now retries 3x with 2s delay between attempts.
5. /api/monitor-status returned false during startup grace — now returns
   alive during grace period if no data has been received yet.
6. Added heartbeat processing time logging to detect slow responses.
7. Added SIGTERM handler for graceful PM2 shutdown logging.

commit.message:
Restore Firebase SDK in SW — required for FCM token registration

getToken() in the app REQUIRES the Firebase messaging SDK to be
present in the service worker. Without it, no push subscription
is created and no pushes are delivered. The "Tap to copy URL"
message was Chrome's PWA install banner, not a push notification.

Key change: our push event listener is registered BEFORE importing
Firebase SDK, so it fires first and controls notification display.
Firebase SDK is loaded after, purely so getToken() works.

Migration bumped to v27 to force fresh SW registration + new FCM
token. App version 2026-03-10e.
